forecasts.reasoning: US presidents rarely make state visits to China in the first 5-6 months of their presidency; historical precedent (for Obama, Trump 1st term, etc.) shows these visits occur later in the year, commonly around November, to coincide with major multilateral summits in Asia (like APEC or the East Asia Summit). Given the knowledge cutoff is just 15 days before the July 1 deadline, any planned trip would likely need to be imminent. Assuming no such trip was part of standard early-term planning, the probability of a surprise or quick state visit to China before July 1, 2025, is extremely low. I am anchoring on this base rate since early US-China diplomacy usually involves the Chinese leader visiting the US first (as Xi did in April 2017) or meeting in a neutral location.
